source: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS) - Safe Minimum Internal Temperatures

chicken breast.visual_cues: Juices run clear, no pink at the bone, firm to the touch
chicken breast.danger_signs: Pink or red juices, rubbery texture, bloody near bone
chicken breast.tips: Use a meat thermometer in the thickest part. Don't rely on color alone.

chicken thigh.visual_cues: Meat pulls easily from bone, juices run clear
chicken thigh.danger_signs: Bloody near the bone, raw appearance
chicken thigh.tips: Thighs are more forgiving than breasts. Cook to 175°F for best texture.

ground beef.danger_signs: Pink center, soft and mushy texture
ground beef.tips: Ground meat must be cooked through — no medium-rare burgers with store-bought ground beef.

beef steak.visual_cues: Firm with slight give for medium-rare, brown crust
beef steak.tips: Rest is essential — juices redistribute. Touch test: press your palm at the base of your thumb.

pork chop.visual_cues: Slight pink in center is safe at 145°F, firm exterior
pork chop.tips: Modern pork is safe at 145°F with rest. Overcooking makes it dry.

pork tenderloin.visual_cues: Light pink center, juicy when sliced
pork tenderloin.tips: Sear first at high heat, finish in oven at 400°F.

salmon.visual_cues: Flakes easily with a fork, translucent to opaque transition
salmon.danger_signs: Completely raw center (unless sushi-grade), dry and flaky (overcooked)
salmon.tips: Many chefs serve at 120-125°F for a silky center. Use sushi-grade for anything under 145°F.

shrimp.tips: Shrimp cook in 2-3 minutes. When they form a C, they're done. O means overdone.

eggs.visual_cues: Whites fully set, yolk thickened (for fully cooked)
eggs.danger_signs: Runny whites (undercooked for serving to vulnerable populations)
eggs.tips: For scrambled eggs, cook until no liquid remains. For fried, cook until whites are set.

rice.danger_signs: Crunchy uncooked center, mushy and clumpy
rice.tips: Leftover rice is a common source of food poisoning (Bacillus cereus). Refrigerate within 1 hour.

pasta.visual_cues: Al dente — tender but with slight bite when tasted
pasta.danger_signs: Hard center (undercooked), mushy and falling apart (overcooked)
pasta.tips: Test often starting 2 minutes before package time. Reserve pasta water — it's liquid gold for sauces.

guidelines.general_advice: All poultry must reach 165°F. No exceptions. Check the thickest part.
Whole cuts are safe at 145°F with 3-minute rest. Surface bacteria are killed by searing.
Ground meats must be cooked through — bacteria gets mixed throughout during grinding.
Fish is done when it flakes easily and reaches 145°F. Shellfish should be opaque.

general.danger_zone: 40°F-140°F (4°C-60°C). Food should not sit in this range for more than 2 hours (1 hour if above 90°F).
general.cross_contamination: Never use the same cutting board for raw meat and ready-to-eat food without washing. Wash hands after handling raw meat.
general.hand_washing: Wash hands with soap and warm water for 20 seconds before and after handling food.
general.storage: Refrigerate leftovers within 2 hours. Use within 3-4 days or freeze.
general.reheating: Reheat leftovers to 165°F. Bring sauces and soups to a rolling boil.
general.thawing: Thaw in the refrigerator, cold water, or microwave — NEVER on the counter at room temperature.
